steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what can be inferred about Ancient Language. An inference must follow directly from information stated in the passage — no outside knowledge, no added assumptions.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Its location may be considered surprising.': The passage says land art artists “usually install large-scale pieces outdoors, often in surprising locations” and identifies Ancient Language as part of the land art movement. Placing it in the Atacama Desert fits that pattern — its location may be considered surprising. ✓
  • Choice B — 'It marked a shift in the popularity of the land art movement.': The passage says nothing about popularity trends before or after 2004. This is outside the text. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It may have had more commercial success in a different outdoor location.': The passage says land art artists reject commercialization — it never discusses commercial success at all. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It was created early in Rogers’s career.': The passage gives no information about when in Rogers’s career 2004 fell. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
